first_paragraph: **Lisa Marie Eilbacher**[[2]](#cite_note-2) (born May 5, 1957)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American former actress. She is best known for her role as Jeanette Summers in *[Beverly Hills Cop](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Cop "Beverly Hills Cop")* (1984).

**Lisa Marie Eilbacher**[[2]](#cite_note-2) (born May 5, 1957)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American former actress. She is best known for her role as Jeanette Summers in *[Beverly Hills Cop](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Cop "Beverly Hills Cop")* (1984).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lisa_Eilbacher&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Eilbacher was born to American parents, Beverly and Robert Charles Eilbacher, in [Dhahran](/wiki/Dhahran "Dhahran"), [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia").[[4]](#cite_note-nb-4) Her father was an executive at [Aramco](/wiki/Aramco "Aramco"), headquartered in Dhahran.[[5]](#cite_note-ai-5) She is the second of five children, two others of whom also worked as child actors.[[4]](#cite_note-nb-4) Eilbacher and her family divided their time between Saudi Arabia, France, Germany, and Switzerland until 1963, when her father relocated the family to [Beverly Hills](/wiki/Beverly_Hills "Beverly Hills") and became a realtor.[[5]](#cite_note-ai-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-mtn-8)[[a]](#cite_note-9) Eilbacher attended Beverly Hills Catholic School and [Beverly Hills High](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_High "Beverly Hills High"). She never formally studied acting, but was coached in it by her mother.[[5]](#cite_note-ai-5) In a 1987 interview, Eilbacher described the coaching she received:

> I really believe that no one can teach anyone to 'act.' You can only be taught to open up, and that she did for me.[[5]](#cite_note-ai-5)

Eilbacher is the older sister of former actress [Cindy Eilbacher](/w/index.php?title=Cindy_Eilbacher&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cindy Eilbacher (page does not exist)"), with whom she appeared in the 1974 television movie *[Bad Ronald](/wiki/Bad_Ronald "Bad Ronald")*,[[9]](#cite_note-deal-10) and of former child actor, [Bobby Eilbacher](/wiki/Bobby_Eilbacher "Bobby Eilbacher").[[4]](#cite_note-nb-4)[[10]](#cite_note-11)[[11]](#cite_note-12)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lisa_Eilbacher&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Child actress and tv roles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lisa_Eilbacher&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Child actress and tv roles")]

Eilbacher started acting as a child, appearing on such shows as *[My Three Sons](/wiki/My_Three_Sons "My Three Sons")* and *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*. She later made a transition into adult roles on such shows as *[The Amazing Spider-Man](/wiki/The_Amazing_Spider-Man_(TV_series) "The Amazing Spider-Man (TV series)")*.

Eilbacher starred as Callie Shaw in *[The Hardy Boys Mysteries](/wiki/The_Hardy_Boys_Mysteries "The Hardy Boys Mysteries")* in 1977. The same year she appeared in the episode "The Innocent" of the short-lived *[Logan's Run](/wiki/Logan%27s_Run_(TV_series) "Logan's Run (TV series)")* TV series. She also guest starred on the TV series *[Man from Atlantis](/wiki/Man_from_Atlantis "Man from Atlantis")* in an episode where she played Shakespeare's Juliet. She also appeared alongside her sister Cynthia "Cindy" Eilbacher at least twice, most famously in the 1974 [made-for-television](/wiki/Made-for-television "Made-for-television") [horror film](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") *[Bad Ronald](/wiki/Bad_Ronald "Bad Ronald")*, in which they portray two of the three teenage daughters of the family newly residing in the old Wilby house.[[9]](#cite_note-deal-10) Prior to that, they had appeared as sisters Bridget and Beth Jordan, onscreen daughters of guest star [Vera Miles](/wiki/Vera_Miles "Vera Miles") in the 1971 *[Alias Smith and Jones](/wiki/Alias_Smith_and_Jones "Alias Smith and Jones")* episode, "The Posse That Wouldn't Quit".[[12]](#cite_note-13)[[13]](#cite_note-14)

### Subsequent roles and films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lisa_Eilbacher&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Subsequent roles and films")]

In 1981, she starred in the television film *[This House Possessed](/wiki/This_House_Possessed "This House Possessed")* with [Parker Stevenson](/wiki/Parker_Stevenson "Parker Stevenson"), with whom she worked on *The Hardy Boys Mysteries*.

That year she acted in [Lee Philips](/wiki/Lee_Philips "Lee Philips")'s film *[On the Right Track](/wiki/On_the_Right_Track "On the Right Track")* starring television star [Gary Coleman](/wiki/Gary_Coleman "Gary Coleman"). Some reviews concluded that it was sappy and capitalizing on Coleman's TV following, many found the film charming, well written, and well acted.[[14]](#cite_note-15)[[15]](#cite_note-16)[[16]](#cite_note-17)[[17]](#cite_note-18)[[18]](#cite_note-19)[[19]](#cite_note-ottreview-20) [Kevin Thomas](/wiki/Kevin_Thomas_(film_critic) "Kevin Thomas (film critic)") of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times"),* felt she stood out by saying it "is Coleman's picture, but its unexpected pleasure is Eilbacher".[[20]](#cite_note-21)

Eilbacher is best known for her roles in two 1980s films: *[An Officer and a Gentleman](/wiki/An_Officer_and_a_Gentleman "An Officer and a Gentleman")* and *[Beverly Hills Cop](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Cop "Beverly Hills Cop")*. In *An Officer and a Gentleman*, she played Naval Aviation Officer Candidate Casey Seeger, a popular and charming woman who nearly flunked out of the program due to her inability to complete the obstacle course; however, she persevered and graduated. An amateur bodybuilder in real life, Eilbacher said the hardest aspect of this role was "pretending" to be out of shape.[[21]](#cite_note-22) In *[Beverly Hills Cop](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Cop "Beverly Hills Cop")*, she played Jenny Summers, a childhood friend of [Eddie Murphy](/wiki/Eddie_Murphy "Eddie Murphy")'s character who reunited with him to solve the murder of a mutual friend.[[22]](#cite_note-23)[[23]](#cite_note-24)

Eilbacher was also featured in the 1983 miniseries *[The Winds of War](/wiki/The_Winds_of_War_(miniseries) "The Winds of War (miniseries)")*, playing Madeleine Henry.[[24]](#cite_note-25)

In 1985, she co-starred with [Holland Taylor](/wiki/Holland_Taylor "Holland Taylor") in the ABC detective series *[Me and Mom](/wiki/Me_and_Mom "Me and Mom")*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lisa_Eilbacher&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Eilbacher has been married to director and photographer Bradford May since 1988.[[1]](#cite_note-cavemancircus.com-1)
